greetingDefault: The Pancreator's grace be upon you, traveller. I am Brother Caspar. How may I serve?
greetingFriendly: Ah, my friend! The light of the Pancreator shines on us both today. What brings you to me?
greetingHostile: I know what you think of me. But the Pancreator teaches patience. Speak your piece.
farewellDefault: Go with the light of the Pancreator. These are dark times — stay wary.
unknownKeywordResponse: I know little of that, I'm afraid. Ask me of my work, my faith, or the people of this city.

keywords.response: I am Brother Caspar — though 'Brother' is more habit than title now. The Church has... distanced itself from my methods.
I tend the sick and hear confessions of those the Church will not visit. The poor, the alien, the outcast. The Pancreator did not distinguish — neither shall I.
The Universal Church is our light in dark times. Though I fear the Institution and the Faith have grown... different things. Inquisitor Harkan is the Institution. I try to be the Faith.
Harkan watches for tech heresy and occult transgression. He is not cruel — he is thorough, which is worse. If you carry anything the Church would call sinful, keep it out of sight at the Spaceport.
I practise the healing rites — Laying on Hands, the Ward. Nothing dramatic. The power comes from faith, not from the Avestite's fire.

Grr'kash works the docks at the Spaceport. A Vorox — enormous, proud, and wiser than people give him credit. He has helped me more than once. And there is a sage in the wilderness, an Ur-Obun elder called Sila. She knows things about this planet that no human text records.

Grr'kash keeps to himself but he's not unfriendly — if you prove you're not afraid of him. He's at the Spaceport docks most mornings. The Pancreator made all peoples, stranger — that includes the Vorox.
Elder Sila lives in the ruins beyond the city walls. She is Ur-Obun — one of the eldest alien peoples. She sees things others cannot. If the old ruins trouble you, seek her.

There is a fever in the lower quarter. Three families now. The city guard calls it nothing. I call it spreading. I need alovera root — grows in the wilderness — to make the remedy. I cannot leave my patients to find it.
Alovera root, ground with salt and boiled with clean water. Simple enough — if you can find the root. It grows near the old stone formations in the wilderness. Will you fetch it for me?
The Pancreator is the one who made all things, and to whom all things return. The suns fade, yes — but even darkness has its place in the Pancreator's design. Or so I tell myself on the hard nights.

Yes. The suns grow dimmer. Every generation the charts record it. The Church says it is the Pancreator's test. Others say it is something we did — or something done to us. I do not know. But it frightens me.
If the Pancreator wills it, I shall accompany you. My patients here are stable for now, and you seem to need someone who can keep you alive. Very well — I am yours to command, within reason.
Go with the light of the Pancreator.
